dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: Clean 3-layer dependency direction: src/segments/ -> src/ -> src/campaigns/. Segments import from core (types, colors, config, effort), campaigns import from core (types), and core (layout.ts) imports
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: 14-file codebase with 3 directories maps cleanly to domain capabilities: src/ (core infrastructure: types, colors, config, layout, entry points), src/segments/ (rendering units: context, model, git, c
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: All functions use camelCase consistently across all 3 directories (campaigns/, src/, segments/). No naming convention drift.
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: Two distinct error strategies are used: (1) silent swallow with fallback behavior (index.ts, config.ts, effort.ts, git.ts catch blocks), (2) log-and-exit in CLI (cli.ts init() catches, logs with conso
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Segment names (contextSegment, modelSegment, gitSegment, campaignSegment, usageSegment) clearly identify their rendering domain
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: The Segment interface has five concrete implementations (context, model, git, campaign, usage), all with meaningfully different render() and enabled() implementations -- genuine polymorphism
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: package.json has zero production dependencies -- the project uses only Node.js built-in modules (child_process, fs, path, os)
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: layout.ts render() is 120 lines mixing brute-force assignment enumeration, row materialization, and rendering -- three distinct concerns in one function body
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: Segment interface defines a clean, uniform contract: id, priority, enabled(data), render(data, width) -> Block. All 5 segments implement this identically, making the handoff from layout.ts to segments
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: Zero test files exist in the entire repository: no *.test.ts, no *.spec.ts, no test/ directory, no __tests__ directory.
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: The primary public API is render(data: StatusLineData, termWidth: number, config: StatusBlocksConfig): string in layout.ts — 3 clear, typed parameters with an obvious return type.
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: This is a terminal status-line renderer — a CLI tool that reads JSON from stdin, formats it with ANSI colors, and writes to stdout. It has no route handlers, no HTTP endpoints, no authentication middl
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: Comment ratio is very low at 0.065 — comments are sparse and mostly purposeful
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: All 14 source files are TypeScript (.ts) — no mixed JS/TS files exist
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: Three directories with clear domain separation: src/ (core infrastructure — 7 files, 537 LOC), src/segments/ (rendering units — 5 files, 292 LOC), src/campaigns/ (promotional rate logic — 2 files, 160
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: Module-level constants are safe: CONFIG_PATH (config.ts:6), SETTINGS_PATH (cli.ts:10), MOCK_DATA (cli.ts:12-24) are all pure derivations from stable values (homedir(), process.cwd()) with no cross-mod
dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: src/campaigns/engine.ts getActiveCampaign() is 88 lines with nested conditionals for weekend/peak/off-peak/upcoming states, mixing time arithmetic, progress calculation, and state classification in a
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: Segment interface contract is consistently honored: all 5 segments implement id, priority, enabled(), render() correctly and return Block objects with the required fields
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: src/segments/campaign.ts lines 37-48: builds 'bar' and 'lines' variables that are computed but never used in the output — dead code path where intermediate results are discarded in favor of 'finalLine
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: StatusLineData interface provides precise typed shapes for all data fields including nested objects, optional fields marked with ?, and union types like 'number | null' for used_percentage

review_issue_summaries.summary: layout.ts hardcodes all segment imports — adding a segment requires editing layout.ts
campaign.ts has dead progress bar logic and contradictory inline comments from iterative development
3 of 5 segments return Block.priority values that differ from their Segment.priority definitions
Campaign segment uses id 'promo' while file is campaign.ts and export is campaignSegment
Top-level catch in index.ts silently swallows all errors, outputting blank line with no diagnostics
Catch blocks inconsistently document their rationale — some have comments, others are bare
campaignSegment export uses id 'promo' creating name/identity mismatch across codebase
resetCountdown sounds like an action (reset the countdown) rather than a formatter
layout.ts render() mixes brute-force layout search, row materialization, and rendering in 120 lines
model.ts 3-part line2 layout uses 9+ local variables for manual gap distribution
campaign.ts render builds bar and lines array then discards them, with stale iteration comments
Layout embeds hardcoded row1 width reduction (maxRowWidth - 5) mixing policy with orchestration
No test framework or test files exist anywhere in the codebase
Campaign engine's timezone-aware date arithmetic has zero test coverage
Most-imported utility (colors.ts, 6 importers) has no tests for ANSI-aware string operations
Combinatorial layout row-assignment algorithm has no test coverage
Segment render() returns Block with priority values contradicting segment's declared priority
truncate() is exported from colors.ts but has no external consumers
Stale thinking-out-loud comments and dead code in campaign.ts from iterative generation
CONFIG_PATH and SETTINGS_PATH bake homedir() at import time, creating untestable stale snapshots
getActiveCampaign() mixes time arithmetic, state classification, and progress calculation in one 88-line body
campaign.ts render() builds bar and lines array then immediately discards them for finalLines
Three segments return Block.priority values that contradict their Segment.priority declarations
campaign.ts render() computes 'bar' progress bar variable that is never included in the output
layout.ts applies an additional unexplained 5-char offset for row 1 beyond the existing INK_PADDING
JSON.parse(input) as StatusLineData casts external stdin input without runtime validation
Environment variable cast to theme union type without validation

dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: Dependency graph is acyclic and layered: src/segments/ depends on src/ and src/campaigns/, src/campaigns/ depends on src/types.ts only, src/ depends on src/segments/ only through layout.ts imports
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: Directory structure maps directly to domain: src/segments/ for rendering units, src/campaigns/ for promotional logic, src/ root for orchestration (index, layout, config, cli) and shared utilities (col
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: All source files use consistent camelCase naming for functions and variables across all directories
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: The codebase uses two error strategies: try-catch-swallow for non-critical operations (terminal width detection, config loading, transcript parsing) and return-null for optional data (getGitInfo, getA
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: campaignSegment export in campaign.ts uses id 'promo', creating a vocabulary split: file name says 'campaign', id says 'promo', DEFAULT_ORDER references 'promo' — developers must know both terms
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: The Segment interface has 5 concrete implementations, all used polymorphically in layout.ts via ALL_SEGMENTS array — this is a genuine abstraction that pays for itself
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: package.json has zero production dependencies — the project uses only Node.js built-in modules (fs, path, os, child_process, Intl)
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: layout.ts render() has been refactored: findOptimalAssignment and materializeAssignment are now extracted, making render() a clean 24-line orchestrator — previously flagged issue is resolved
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: Segment interface provides a clean contract: each segment implements id, priority, enabled(), render() and returns a Block — layout.ts consumes these uniformly without per-segment conditionals
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: Zero test files exist: no *.test.ts, *.spec.ts, or __tests__/ directories anywhere in the repository
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: All 5 segments implement the same Segment interface consistently: id (string), priority (number), enabled(data) (boolean), render(data, allocWidth) (Block) — uniform API shape across siblings
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: This is a CLI status-line renderer — it reads JSON from stdin and writes ANSI-formatted text to stdout. There are no HTTP routes, no API endpoints, no user authentication, no database access, and no p
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: Code is purpose-driven with minimal ceremony across all 14 files
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: All 14 source files are TypeScript — no mixed JS/TS modules
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: Three directories with clear purpose: src/ (core infrastructure), src/segments/ (rendering units), src/campaigns/ (promotional rate logic)

dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: getActiveCampaign() in engine.ts (88 lines) mixes time-zone resolution, day-of-week classification, peak/off-peak state determination, countdown calculation, and progress computation in a single funct
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: All five segments correctly return Block objects matching their declared Segment.priority values (previously fixed: model=30, campaign=20, usage=15, context=10, git=40)
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: campaign.ts computes barWidth (line 37) and constructs a lines array (lines 38-41) that is used only for width calculation (line 43) — the barWidth variable name is misleading since it is not actually
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: index.ts line 14: JSON.parse(input) as StatusLineData — casts external stdin input without any runtime validation. If Claude Code sends malformed JSON or a different schema, the code will throw at arb
